items.title: Court hears former AFL footballer Nick Stevens likely to face prison term

children.content: A Victorian court hears former AFL player Nick Stevens was a blue-collar worker but committed a white-collar crime, ahead of his sentencing for defrauding six families in 2017.

gallery.altText: A sketch of a bald Caucasian man in prison green

thumbnail.altText: A sketch of a bald Caucasian man in prison green

items.title: Passenger denies being uncooperative as Avalon Airport evacuated

children.content: A traveller has been released without charge after four hours in police custody after a security scare at the airport early this morning.

image.altText: A man with long wavy dark hair and glasses in a dark jacket and light brown shirt stares off camera.

items.title: Coles investigates 'weird scratching' Apple gift card scam complaint

children.content: Coles is looking into a Melbourne mother's complaint about faulty Apple gift cards worth $2,000.

image.altText: A close up of an Apple gift card with the numbers scratched off.

items.title: McDonald's circles Castlemaine but can expect a fight over its 'soul'

children.content: McDonald's says it has identified a site in Castlemaine and is exploring the possibility of establishing a restaurant, but it's not being welcomed by some in the town.

image.altText: A vcant clock of lands sits behind a historic statue of a gold miner

items.title: Hawks charge back as Mitchell fumes after rapid Crows start

children.content: Hawthorn returns to Tasmania to host Adelaide just days after being told it will have to cease playing home games in the state by 2028. Follow live.

image.altText: Callum Ah Chee celebrates with Adelaide Crows teammates

items.title: Female AFL creators say vile comments 'just one example of what we deal with'

children.content: A video circulated across social media of a group of men laughing about how one of them would "beat the f***" out of a group of female content creators.

articles.title: AFLW's 2026 fixture to feature women's and men's double-headers

children.content: A series of double-headers involving the women's and men's competitions will take place after the AFLW season begins in August.

image.altText: A St Kilda AFLW player kicks the ball as a Carlton opponent looks to smother.

gallery.altText: A screen shot of The Jumper Punch podcasters during an episode.

thumbnail.altText: A screen shot of The Jumper Punch podcasters during an episode.

items.title: Kylie Minogue reveals 'secret' cancer and IVF struggles in Netflix doco

children.content: "Our Kylie" gets candid in the three-part Netflix docuseries, grieving over IVF struggles, Michael Hutchence, and revealing she was diagnosed with cancer a second time in 2021.

image.altText: Close-up portrait of pop star Kylie Minogue on red carpet

items.title: Victoria market to redirect 800 tonnes of food waste to hungry Melburnians

children.content: A new program at Melbourne's Queen Victoria Market is tackling the massive amount of food wasted, while helping the rising number of people struggling to put food on the table.

image.altText: boxes of fresh produce next to each other, including capsicum, broccoli and eggplant.

items.title: Delays could see nation's first offshore wind project blown off course

children.content: Environmental timelines outlined by Star of the South show the offshore wind farm project could take up to an additional five years to be completed, leaving some in the energy sector wondering whether Victoria could suffer an energy shortfall.

image.altText: A series of wind turbines in the ocean.

items.title: Pendlebury's 433rd game simply his latest of many AFL records

children.content: Scott Pendlebury is set to be the proud holder of the AFL's games record, but it's only the latest in a long line of records held by the Collingwood icon.

image.altText: Scott Pendlebury clenches his fist in celebration of a goal

items.title: Dairy factory's 'very sad' departure leaves residents wondering about town's future

children.content: Bega Cheese's Strathmerton factory, which employed more than 300 workers, is set to close its doors on June 30, leaving residents in the small Victorian town wondering what's next.

gallery.altText: A woman stands outside the front of a brick building with a sign saying Strathmerton bakery

thumbnail.altText: A woman stands outside the front of a brick building with a sign saying Strathmerton bakery

items.title: Lawyers say teen accused of trying to hijack plane was mentally impaired

children.content: A teen who allegedly tried to hijack a plane at Avalon Airport felt relieved when passengers restrained him, describing it as a "weight off his shoulders", his lawyers say.

gallery.altText: Several people holding down a teenage boy in an aeroplane

thumbnail.altText: Several people holding down a teenage boy in an aeroplane

avatar.alt: A man with dark skin in a suit looks at the camera

items.title: Luxury apartments greenlit under affordable housing scheme

children.content: Property developers are using an affordable housing scheme to bypass normal planning requirements and win fast-tracked approval for contentious projects across Melbourne.

image.altText: An artist's impression of a multi-story building development, alongside a busy road.

items.title: Melbourne recycling plant pumping out 'fermenting' smell, locals say

children.content: A urban planning expert says Victoria's industrial zoning laws are outdated and that operations like Visy's paper recycling plant in Reservoir may no longer be appropriate so close to housing.

image.altText: A sign that says "Visy" on a wire fence across the road from houses.

items.title: Teenaged trio arrested over Melbourne nightclub arsons

children.content: Victoria Police allege the three males are responsible for arson attacks at nightclubs La Di Da and Electric Bar earlier this month.

image.altText: A man being arrested by two people

items.title: Fisheries body pushes for carp virus trial in Victoria's Wimmera

children.content: The Victorian Fisheries Authority wants to host a carp virus trial, but the federal government says it needs regulatory approval, which will delay research.

image.altText: Several carp at gasping for air at the end of the Pamamaroo inlet at the Menindee Lakes

items.title: Teenager Bianca Adler becomes youngest Australian to summit Everest

children.content: Not many 18-year-olds scale the world's highest mountain midway through year 12. But Bianca Adler has pulled it off, and is now undertaking the tricky descent.

gallery.altText: A girl wearing a helmet with stickets in a purple jacket holds a pick ice pick close to the camera, smiling widely.
A girl with a grey beanie, red scarf high up under her chin and a jacket inside a tent holding a mug with liquid.

items.title: 'Backbone' of Victoria's bushfire, flood response to be cut, union claims

children.content: The State Control Centre, which responds to natural disasters like bushfires and floods, is to shed around two thirds of its full-time equivalent staff according to the Community and Public Sector Union.

image.altText: a white car has been completely burnt out by a fire

items.title: Two dead after speeding car hits tree in Melbourne's south-east

children.content: Police say a car was travelling along the Princes Highway when the driver lost control and crashed into a tree about 1:20am.

image.altText: A firefighter in overalls and a helmet holds a hose near the wreckage of a badly damaged silver coloured car at night.

items.title: Man dead after multi-vehicle crash near Victoria-NSW border

children.content: NSW Police say a van had rear-ended a vehicle, causing the vehicle to collide with a B-double truck travelling the opposite way.

image.altText: road closed

items.title: The battle for Tasmania's north has been won, but locals aren't convinced

children.content: They may not be scheduled to play their first AFL game until 2028, but the Tasmania Devils have claimed their first scalp — booting Hawthorn out of the state which has been its second home for more than two decades.

image.altText: Two football players celebrate during a game.

items.title: Six decades on and this Victorian community is still seeking its new bridge

children.content: Businesses relying on heavy transport to move goods across the Victoria-NSW border near Swan Hill say they are growing frustrated at the continual delays in replacing a 130-year-old wooden bridge.

gallery.altText: drone shot of swan hill bridge

thumbnail.altText: A dark blue car approaches the Swan Hill timber bridge with a red truck crossing it.
drone shot of swan hill bridge

items.title: Australia is wasting $1 billion on car parks nobody uses, report finds

children.content: Australia is wasting more than $1 billion each year building off-street car parks that end up unused and unwanted, a Grattan report has found.

image.altText: A woman holding her toddler son's hand walk through a car parking garage that is about half empty.

items.title: Tour company fallout deepens as more travellers speak out

children.content: The ABC has been contacted by more than 100 travellers since first reporting on the matter on Monday, with some claiming their tours were cancelled at the last minute and itineraries changed.

image.altText: A man standing with NYC behind him.

items.title: Gippsland Nationals MP Tim Bull to exit politics at Victorian election

children.content: Tim Bull has announced he will not contest the November election, after representing Gippsland East for the National Party for 16 years.

image.altText: A middle-aged man in a suit answering questions from journalists at a press conference

items.title: Hawthorn 'extremely disappointed' by AFL's call to end Tasmanian partnership

children.content: Hawthorn is less than pleased after being forced to cease playing home games in Tasmania ahead of the Devils' AFL entry.

image.altText: Jai Newcombe holding a football in his right hand

items.title: Boomers star Joe Ingles thrilled to come 'home' in coup NBL signing

children.content: The legendary Boomers and NBA star will return to Australia's NBL for the first time since 2009, signing with Melbourne United for two years.

image.altText: Joe Ingles smiles with a towel around his neck

items.title: 'Morale is low': Grocery, butcher's set alight in Melbourne shopping strip

children.content: Fire crews were called to a blaze in Glenroy that gutted several shops. Locals say there have been multiple suspicious fires in the area.

image.altText: Police tape cordons off an area in the front of burnt shops.

items.title: See where climate change is hitting Victoria's infrastructure hardest

children.content: At least $57 billion worth of infrastructure in Victoria is vulnerable to more frequent and severe bushfires, floods and extreme heat. This figure is expected to grow.

items.title: What Kylie Minogue learned from Michael Hutchence and Nick Cave

children.content: Australia's reigning Queen of Pop discusses her relationships with Nick Cave and the late Michael Hutchence, and being tapped to headline the AFL grand final's pre-game entertainment.

image.altText: pop star Kylie Minogue sits in a dressing gown on a deck chair smiling pensively.

items.title: 'Two years to live': Why we used our super for weight-loss surgery

children.content: Weight-loss surgery has become an increasingly common option for people grappling with obesity — and many are funding it using retirement savings.

image.altText: A man with glasses, a white t-shirt and a sleeve tattoo, stands beside a woman with glasses and a dark blue top.

items.title: Man who moved fridge with Isla Bell's body inside once jailed for assault

children.content: The ABC can reveal Eyal Yaffe previously served time for assaulting and choking a woman who he threatened to "stuff in a suitcase".

image.altText: A bald man with a short grey beard in a cream shirt uses crutches to move down a street beside a sandstone building.

items.title: Cancelled pianist denies misleading MSO on Gaza performance as trial begins

children.content: Pianist Jayson Gillham is suing the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra in the Federal Court over political discrimination after comments he made about Gaza on stage during a 2024 performance.

image.altText: Jayson Gillham sits on a step outside in a professional portrait photo

items.title: Community fights back against caravan park development proposal

children.content: A community group in Victoria's south-east will appeal a council planning decision to approve the development of a regional caravan park, which it says would be an "absolute disaster" for the region.

items.title: Plans for largest wind farm in Southern Hemisphere get Victorian approval

children.content: An environmental advocacy group and a council mayor say the Victorian government has not properly considered the potential impacts of building a major new wind farm in Western Victoria.
